description_long: This object is an old-style CRT computer monitor with a bulky, angular casing and a prominent, deep back section for housing the cathode ray tube. The front features a large, slightly recessed glass display surrounded by a thick, rectangular bezel with visible wear and minor scuff marks. Ventilation grilles are present on both sides and the rear, indicating heat dissipation needs typical of CRT technology. The base is a flat, rectangular stand that supports the monitor's weight and provides stability. Several small, round buttons are located on the lower right of the front bezel, likely for power and display adjustments. The overall color is a worn, off-white or light gray, with some darker accents in the vent areas and screen.
description: A bulky, off-white CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, deep rear casing, side and rear vents, and a flat rectangular stand.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, angular rear casing and horizontal ventilation grilles.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the large glass screen, thick bezel, and a row of small buttons on the lower right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the deep casing and side vents seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the broad, flat back with a large vent grille near the bottom.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and made of matte off-white plastic with visible wear and minor scuffs. It has sharp corners and no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: Flat, rectangular pedestal base made of the same off-white plastic as the casing, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A row of small, round buttons is located on the lower right edge of the front bezel, grouped together.
